change: Export owner-resolved live call trees and tick inventories
exact_area: combat_analyze.py plus Oasis/Purification II combat runs
why: Current data stops at inclusive UWorld/component/animation/Blueprint timers; it cannot name the actor class owning the remaining milliseconds.
implementation: Keep the full gt_*.csv outputs, add object/class-labelled TRACE_CPUPROFILER scopes around AI Tick, visibility, skill TickComponent, movement and player target detection; capture `dumpticks grouped` at 60 s intervals.
target: At least 90% of active GT assigned to named exclusive or parent/child-resolved owners.
ship_gate: Do not claim a one-class root cause until owner-resolved evidence exists.

why: CanStopTick currently returns bHitFeedbackAlive after proving all DoT maps are empty. The caller disables tick only when that function is true, so completed hit feedback (alive=false) can never stop the component; final DoT removal also never retries shutdown.
implementation: Return `!bHitFeedbackAlive && !bHitFeedbackTick` after the DoT loop. Add a shared TryStopTick() and call it after the final DoT removal and after hit-feedback completion.
target: Zero StatManager components ticking with no DoT, hit-feedback fade or other timed work pending.
ship_gate: Automated hit-only, DoT-only, overlapping hit+DoT and time-dilation tests; tick always stops after the final state clears.

why: Activating an AI enables every skill component, while the base and AI skill ticks mostly poll booleans and empty queues. That fan-out scales with active enemy count and is consistent with the ~4.1 ms live actor/component timer.
implementation: Add RefreshTickEnabled() and enable a skill tick only while cooldown, firerate, duration, multihit, FX queue, multiraycast, recovery, preshoot or delayed-area work is active. Re-enable at each state transition; disable immediately when the last state clears.
target: Reduce component-tick calls by at least 30%; historical City target ≤60 calls/frame and ≤2.5 ms component tick; save 0.7–1.5 ms active GT without fire-rate/cooldown drift.
ship_gate: Deterministic combat seed: identical shots, damage, kills and cooldown completion within one frame.

change: Batch enemy compass updates instead of ticking one component per icon
why: Seeking/attacking enemies register compass locators that tick every frame. Each icon update repeats distance/projection/transform/curve/UMG setter work, matching the combat run's high component-dispatch count and ~1.0–1.3 ms Slate RenderThread cost.
implementation: Replace per-locator ticks with one controller/widget registry updated at 30 Hz for central threats and 20 Hz for distant/offscreen icons. Cache camera transform and viewport once per batch, early-out by squared distance, and skip UMG setters unless value deltas exceed a visual threshold.
target: Remove one tick function per registered compass icon; cut compass CPU ≥50% and lower Slate RenderThread by ≥0.3 ms in historical-City-equivalent combat.
ship_gate: No visible judder during head turns; offscreen warnings react within 50 ms; icons unregister correctly on death/travel.

exact_area: ACharacter_AI dynamic-loading state, controller, brain and path-following activation
why: The existing Near/Far switch is binary: Near enables actor, controller, brain, path following and skills at full cadence. Oasis and Purification II remain CPU-dominant even with very different kill counts.
implementation: Keep bosses, the current attacker/target and enemies within 1,500 uu at 72 Hz; update non-attacking visible Near AI at 36 Hz; update off-axis or occluded Near AI at 24 Hz; leave Far AI disabled as today. Stagger phase offsets by UniqueID.
target: Actor/component inclusive timer ≤3.0 ms and active GT ≤10.5 ms in both case studies.
ship_gate: No navigation overshoot, delayed attacks, hit-regression or animation/root-motion divergence in replayed combat.

change: Make player target detection fixed-rate and cache the broad phase
why: DetectTargets runs every frame and performs a capsule sweep, per-cone sorting, per-impact visibility traces and two crosshair raycasts. Frozen controls already spend ~0.18–0.20 ms/frame before live enemy density.
implementation: Run the broad-phase sweep at 36 Hz (18 Hz when no candidate is nearby), reuse candidates between sweeps, and retain a cheap per-frame ray only for the currently selected target. Avoid sorting all hits; partial-select the closest candidate per cone.
target: STAT_DetectTargets ≤0.10 ms median and ≤0.25 ms P99 in live Oasis/Purification II.
ship_gate: Target acquisition latency ≤27.8 ms and no aim-assist/focus regressions under dual-wield and teleport tests.

exact_area: ACharacter_AI mesh policy, GraphicsSettingsSubsystem and health-bar widget components
why: Skinned-mesh work is ~0.89–0.91 ms/frame in both CPU cases. Existing URO and 20 Hz widget logic are directionally correct but still leave nearly a millisecond of animation work.
implementation: Preserve full-rate pose/bone refresh only for rendered combatants and the local player; use URO 2/3/4-frame rates by screen size for other enemies; disable health-bar widget tick/render targets when occluded or outside display range rather than merely running at 20 Hz.
target: Skinned-mesh inclusive timer ≤0.60 ms and zero hidden health-bar render-target updates.
ship_gate: No visible pose pops, stale hit bones, floating bars or root-motion divergence in headset capture.

change: Convert the hottest Blueprint ReceiveTick owners after they are named
exact_area: Blueprint Events / ReceiveTick owners from the P0 call-tree export
why: Blueprint events are ~0.72–0.78 ms/frame, but the artifact does not identify which Blueprint classes own them.
implementation: For the top two owners, replace Tick with delegates/timers, set explicit tick intervals, or move the stable hot loop to C++. Do not bulk-convert Blueprints without owner evidence.
target: Blueprint event timer ≤0.45 ms in both levels.
ship_gate: Same event counts and state transitions in an instrumented before/after replay.

change: Reduce CPU render submission after GameThread work
exact_area: visibility/proxy policy for Purification_Water_1; RHI Translate, InitViews and BasePass
why: Purification II also carries the highest live RHI Translate value (6.814 ms) and 1.617 ms BasePass among the live levels. Frozen spot A is GPU-heavy while spot B is healthy, so this is view-local rather than a reason to globally lower resolution.
implementation: Use the A/B view to tighten HISM/proxy coverage, cull occluded vegetation/room pieces and reduce material sections for the visible high-draw cluster. Preserve the existing eye resolution until CPU work is fixed.
target: RHI Translate −1.0 ms and BasePass −0.4 ms in the problematic view; no regression in spot B.
ship_gate: 72 Hz at both deterministic spots and unchanged gameplay collision/navigation.

change: Diagnose hitches as a separate root-cause track
exact_area: frames above 35 ms with load-time, GC, physics and PSO trace channels
why: Oasis reaches 214.94 ms and Purification II 281.89 ms, but the published hover explanations are heuristics, not per-frame call stacks.
implementation: Recapture the worst 10 seconds with file/asset loading, memory/GC, physics and PSO events; correlate each hitch timestamp before changing streaming pools or caches.
target: P99 ≤20 ms and zero frames above 50 ms after a root-cause-specific fix.
ship_gate: Three cold boots and three warm boots; report cold/warm separately.
